body:
Two accounting defects in `crates/custodian/src/reconstruction.rs` (verified on `main` @ `82be6ae`):

**1. `reconstruction_repaired` over-counts (`reconstruction.rs:163-172`, `:434-436`).** `emit_repaired` fires **upfront for every plan** before the rebuild/commit loop. The doc comment at `:432-433` states "successful repairs are `reconstruction_repaired − conflict`", but the `RepairOutcome::Aborted` arm (`:172`) is offset by **nothing** (only `Conflict` increments `reconstruction_conflict`). So `reconstruction_repaired − reconstruction_conflict` over-counts true successes by the number of Aborted plans.

**2. `time_to_repair` is an absolute instant, not an elapsed window (`reconstruction.rs:436`).** `histogram.reconstruction_time_to_repair_millis = now_millis` records the logical instant of the pass, not elapsed repair time. Self-flagged at `:427-431` as a later refinement; filing so it stays tracked.

**Suggested fix.** Either emit `reconstruction_repaired` only on `Committed`, or add a `reconstruction_aborted` counter so the success identity holds; carry a per-obligation enqueue stamp for a true elapsed `time_to_repair`.

**Refs:** #144 (M3.6); proposal 0005 §326-332.

milestone.title: M3 — Custodians
milestone.description: The self-maintaining durability plane (L4): GC, scrub, reconstruction, rebalance + durability telemetry. Makes single-zone trustworthy; gates the Step-2 release. Plan: proposal 0005.

title: Reconstruction telemetry: time_to_repair is an absolute instant, and reconstruction_repaired over-counts successes by the Aborted count
